Candy Corn is not your average dog! This handsome boy stands out with his incredibly unique eyes and coat—a striking brindle pattern set against an orange hue, earning him his sweet and tasty name.

He is a delightful companion who brings joy wherever he goes with his infectious smile.. Candy Corn is very housetrained and leash-trained, making him a breeze to live with. As a true Lab, he adores food and treats (which makes further training fun!).

He’s ready for fun with his favorite activity being fetch, which he’ll happily play until you tire out. Candy Corn is a playful, loving, and unique pup ready to shower his forever family with affection!


Interested in Candy Corn? reach out to his friends at orangedotcrew@gmail.com for more info!

name: Canela
description_html: Canela has such a wonderful personality and is such a sweet girl. She’s playful and loves fetch with her stuffies (she’s pretty good at dropping them for you to toss too!). She also loves playing in the little puppy pools, and splashing around in them. She’s also very house trained! Once she works out her zoomies, she’s super easy on a leash. She’s a super cuddly young pup, and loves to lean against her human for pets and scratches.

name: Evermore
description_html:
Evermore is a very good boy who walks well on leash and will "sit" for a treat. He is housetrained and crate trained, as well. Evermore is affectionate with people and loves cuddles!


Evermore is a sweet, calm and gentle boy. He’s also one of the most housetrained dogs at the shelter. He does well meeting new people and he seems comfortable being around other dogs. He has lived with two different dogs in foster matching their vibes (one younger more playful and the other a senior professional relaxer!). Evermore has the potential to live with another medium/large dog and would require a meet and greet with the behavior team at AAS.


Evermore has done well on outings and in new environments. He takes it all in but will also sit at your feet while you enjoy a coffee in the sunshine.


Evermore loves to get out and explore, walking and sniffing like a champion. He also happily rides in the car, windows down if possible for the best sniffs!

He likes to play with toys and will play ball and with ropes toys especially. As well as being a great adventure buddy Evermore loves to relax and chill at home. A wonderful couch buddy!

name: Hendrix
description_html:
Hendrix is a two-year-old heartthrob with one goal in life: to love his person with his entire soul. He’s fully house-trained, incredibly smart, and very treat-motivated (which means training with him is basically just a snack-based friendship).


His favorite “job” at the shelter is visiting the real-life rooms on campus with volunteers, where he takes his snuggling very seriously. Couch? Claimed. Lap? Claimed. Emotional support duties? Nailed it. Hendrix doesn’t just cuddle — he commits.


This boy is affectionate, gentle, and deeply people-oriented. He’s the kind of dog who looks at you like you’re his whole world… because you are. Once Hendrix picks his person, that’s it — you’ve found your soul dog.


If you’re searching for a loyal best friend, a professional snuggler, and a dog who will love you harder than you thought possible, Hendrix is ready and waiting. Email orangedotcrew@gmail.com to learn more and kickstart this heartwarming journey! For more pupdates, follow Orange Dot Crew on Facebook and Instagram.

name: Apple Fritter
description_html:
Apple Fritter is the perfect mix of sweetness and smarts! This loving, cuddly boy adores being close to his people - whether it’s snuggling on the couch or leaning in for a hug. He’s a great listener and eager to please, making him a joy to have around.


Apple Fritter is both crate trained and house trained, so he’s already mastered his manners. He loves attention but also knows how to relax quietly when it’s time to wind down. He would do best in a home where his owners are home the majority of the time, as he truly thrives on companionship and close connection.


If you’re looking for a loyal companion who will shower you with affection and make every day brighter, Apple Fritter is your guy!

name: Chewy
description_html:
Between his beautiful merle coat, pointy ears, and soft brown eyes Chewy quickly became a staff/volunteer favorite! Chewy likes to meet new humans gently but is cuddly and so so wiggly with his friends. The quickest way to Chewy’s heart is toys, hotdogs, more toys, and more hotdogs!


Chewy’s great leash manners make him a perfect walking buddy and his adorable zoomies in the yard are always full of charm. Chewy’s staff friends describe him as “so dang precious” and “so sweet.”


Have a dog at home? Chewy goes to playgroup at the shelter and is labeled as a solid coexister aka a real chill guy.


Ready to add some love to your life? Chewy is certain to put a smile on your face. Visit him at Austin Animal Services today!

name: Dom
description_html:
Dom is a fun, playful, and incredibly handsome boy who’s ready to find his forever home. He’s easy to harness, and once you toss a toy out of his kennel, he’s all in and ready to go! Dom can be strong at first on leash, but after he gets some energy out, he settles in nicely and relaxes.

Dom is very smart and listens well—he already knows tricks and is eager to learn more, especially when food and treats are involved (they’re his favorite!). He absolutely loves spending time playing in the yard, showing off his playful and goofy side.

With a medium energy level, Dom would be a great match for an active lifestyle, and he’ll likely relax even more once he’s out of the shelter environment. He’s leash trained, house trained, and full of personality—lovable, intelligent, and always ready for fun.


Interested in Dom? Reach out to his friends at orangedotcrew@gmail.com for more info!

name: Nova
description_html:
Nova is one of the lucky dogs participating in our new program Enduring Service! This program pairs a shelter dog with inmates at the Travis State Jail to complete a 12-week basic training course. Upon graduation, they will receive a course certificate of completion and a goodie bag to go home with their adoptive family.


Nova is not currently onsite at the shelter but to learn more about her and how to adopt, select the “Get Started” button to hear from a staff member! 


Nova is a wonderful mature dog with a loving heart and a gentle spirit. She's both fun and easy to train, making her a great companion for a variety of homes. Nova enjoys staying active, she loves to run and her favorite game is chase but she also has a calm, relaxed side and carries herself with a ladylike temperament. She's an excellent walking or jogging partner and walks politely by your side. Nova is smart, loyal, and well-trained. She has mastered basic obedience commands using both voice and hand signals, including sit, stay, down, heel, and more. She is kennel trained and comfortable resting in her crate both during the day and overnight. She even knows a few fun tricks like roll over! Nova is friendly and enjoys meeting new people and dogs. She may bark to alert you to strangers or unfamiliar situations, but she warms up with time and proper introductions. Once she feels comfortable, her sweet and affectionate personality truly shines. Nova is a loving, loyal girl who is ready to find her forever family-someone who will appreciate her intelligence, energy, and big heart.

name: Abner
description_html:
Abner is a handsome and sweet dog who is ready to steal your heart! This stunning boy has a truly unforgettable look, thanks to his striking eyes—one bright blue and one warm brown. His unique gaze perfectly matches his wonderful personality. 

On a walk, Abner is a dream, demonstrating excellent manners and staying easy on the leash. When it's time to play, he's energetic and engaging, but he settles down just as quickly for some quality snuggle time. Above all, Abner is affectionate and believes there is no problem a good, long belly rub can't solve. If you're looking for a playful, loving companion with an undeniably captivating look, Abner is the perfect match!


Interested in Abner? Reach out to his friends at orangedotcrew@gmail.com for more info!

name: Loki
description_html:
Loki is a very cute fun puppy. He has some energy and isn't burning it at the shelter, but is delightful in the play yard. He loves chasing toys and getting his zoomies out. He can be a little more excited on leash, but it's nothing crazy. Just toss a toy in front to keep him moving as he learns leash life. He does better after he can burn off his puppiness and will do great in a home that can help him decompress.


Interested in Loki? Reach out to his friends at orangedotcrew@gmail.com for more info!

name: Bear
description_html:
Follow Bear's adventures on Instagram: @adopt_bestboybear


If you are looking for a loyal shadow, a world-class snuggler, and a dog who truly tunes into your guidance, Bear is your boy. At 60 lbs, he genuinely believes he’s a lap-sized "little love bug," and once you are in his circle, you are in for life.


What's Bear made up of? Wisdom Panel: www.wisdompanel.com/app/s/52slwdf


Bear was dumped in th streets, which left him with a little bit of lingering fear. Because of this, he’s a "90% perfect" boy who just needs a semi-experienced handler to help him conquer that last 10%. He looks to his person for direction, recovers quickly when startled, and walks beautifully on a leash. He’s incredibly smart, highly food-motivated, knows a ton of commands, and boasts great recall. He is not reactive by nature and is the type of boy that sits by your feet at a brewery or outside eatery! If this boy wasn't so conflicted about cats, his foster would adopt him in a second.


Bear’s Ideal Home Checklist:

  • The Humans: A moderately active household, bear enjoys a daily walk and for someone to throw his toys. Older kids are fine, but tiny kiddos probably aren't the right match for him. However he’s not been tested with them.
  • The Setup: A house rather than an apartment. Bear has some mild separation anxiety and gets really excited when you return. Don't worry—you won't be a prisoner in your home! His foster parent successfully works 8–10 hour days, but an apartment wall might be a bit too close for comfort while he continues to work through vocalizing his happiness and sadness.
  • The Pack: Bear loves having a canine sibling to copy! He currently thrives living with small dogs (he adores snuggling with his chug sister) and does great with dogs his size who have a calm, chill vibe. Larger or high-energy dogs can intimidate him, but with proper introductions, he can find his groove with almost anyone. Not sure a dog park would be right for him as he prefers to take a minute to get to know someone, but based on his behavior he could eventually be that "off leash BFF" for you.
  • No Felines: While he currently lives with a cat, he finds them a bit too conflicting to happily coexist, so a cat-free home is best.


Whether you want to go hiking, take a car ride, visit a local brewery, or just chill out on the couch with a 61 lb weighted blanket at your feet, Bear is ready to be your ultimate companion. He just needs a human who will stand up for him, protect his boundaries, and keep showing him that the world isn’t so scary after all.



Follow Bear's adventures on Instagram: @adopt_bestboybear

name: Tiger
description_html: Tiger’s foster says: "Tiger is a really sweet cuddly guy. He is wonderful with all people. He is really sweet with kids. We have a five-year-old who has tested this extensively and I’ve never even seen him nip at her or even growl. He has some leash reactivity with other dogs sometimes but he doesn’t always bark at all dogs. I have not socialized him with other dogs so I’m not sure that going to a dog park or a heavily dog trafficked area would be a good idea for him. He is really a sweet, sweet, loving guy."

name: Filo
description_html:
Meet Filo, a Boxer/Lab mix with the rare trifecta: he’s lived successfully with big dogs, small dogs, a cat, and older children. His previous family described him as friendly, playful, lovable, potty trained, crate trained, and easy to handle — and he’s continued to show those same wonderful qualities in the shelter.


Filo is super housetrained and great with toys (fetch is his love language — he’ll even drop the ball right into your hand). He has a soft, social play style with other dogs and is affectionate, cuddly, and gentle once he knows you. He’s easy to leash, easy to walk, and easy to re-kennel, making him a joy to handle day to day. He’s also both food- and fun-motivated — the perfect combination for ongoing training and bonding.


Filo forms strong connections with his people and truly thrives in a calm, predictable environment. He can be uneasy around unfamiliar people and may need time and space to feel comfortable with new faces. Because of this, he’s looking for a low-traffic home with minimal visitors, where he can feel safe and relaxed. With patience and consistency, he continues to build confidence and show more of his sweet, playful personality.


Come meet Filo — he’s the kind of dog who makes a home feel warmer the minute he walks in, and for the right person, he’ll be an incredibly loyal and rewarding companion.

name: Thalia
description_html:
Thalia might be the snuggliest on site. She will roll around in your lap until every body part is rubbed and kissed. She is a people lover and happy girl. She has a gorgeous smile and upbeat personality. She likes to play and bounce around but would prefer to wiggle on top of you or next to you. This is why the lap is her favorite.


See my IG here: https://www.instagram.com/adopt.thalia.atx?igsh=N2xvaTFpcjJzbTN5&utm_source=qr

name: Meatball
description_html:
His name may be Meatball, but this guy is actually a total ham! This sweet, snuggly boy is famous for his full-body cuddles and big goofy grin.

Don’t let his laid-back charm fool you. Meatball isn’t just lazy! He’s playful, loves zooming around the yard, and then happily settles in for some serious snuggle time. He’s a mostly loose-leash walker and an extremely house-trained gentleman who knows how to keep things neat and tidy.

True heeler intelligence. He got all the treats out of a puzzle gently within 3 seconds flat of setting it down. Very good about placing right in front of you when you call him, impressive. He could probably be someone's champ agility dog! A cuddly heeler is a true rarity, what a gem! Loves to give kisses and lean, and rub against you. Such a sweetheart.

With his mix of sweetness, silliness, and perfectly portioned energy, Meatball is the ultimate comfort companion. Come meet him and see why everyone falls in love with this lovable ham!

name: Cherry Pie
description_html:
Cherry Pie is a super shy girl that is still learning to trust people. She was abandoned with a couple of cat friends and she seems to get a lot of comfort from other cats so we recommend that she go to a home with a friendly cat companion.

Cherry Pie is not yet comfortable with people petting her, but may allow you to scratch her with one of our special cat scratchers. She will need a very patient, cat savvy adopter who is familiar with socialization and will give her the space and time she needs to transition to a new home.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Cherry Pie and ALL Desperate Housecats/Cactus Cats!

name: Petey
description_html:
Petey is a little guy with a big heart. He loves his people and would love nothing more than a house with lots of toys and a spot on the couch. Or, the floor. Or anywhere. Petey is known to be able to fall asleep on any surface and in any position. But, his favorite spot will always be near his people.


Petey walks great on a leash and can self entertain with toys. He seems to prefer chewing on hard toys but would probably enjoy a variety. He loves to be pet and gets occasional zoomies. A volunteer who took him on an outing to her home noted that he loves car rides and curled up immediately and fell asleep in the backseat. She also gave him up a thumbs up for no accidents in the house and very polite manners. He won’t get on the couch unless invited.


Petey has lived with another dog at the shelter and is known as a co-exister in play group. He could go home with a well-matched dog after a meet and greet.


Whether he’s an only pet or is joining your pack, Petey will be a sweet and gentle companion who would love to fall asleep in your lap.

name: Fendi
description_html:
Meet a gentle, shy lady who pours her heart and soul into everyone she encounters. This nurturing nature has unfortunately led to her being shuffled around, resulting in her arriving back to the shelter again, in need of a stable home. She's believes in the goodness of people and she's ready to move forward with so much to offer;

she is housetrained, crate trained, and remains calm and content in her surroundings, always radiating her sweet charm.

She enjoys the company of some dogs. Let's set up a meet with your dog.

See her short video here and here

Fendi is advocated for by her friends at ClassicCaninesATX - stop by to meet her or email caring@classiccanines.org with questions.

name: Yerba
description_html:
Yerba and Tea came in as silly little stray scaredy kittens. They are on a journey of self-reflection. Their goal is to be braver with people! Yerba and Tea enjoy play time with wand toys, and though they may take some time to warm up to new people, we think they will reach their goal sooner than later. They may do best in a calm home with a cat friend to learn from and a patient adopter to help them on their journey. Yerba and Tea are now a part of our Cactus Cat program for shy cats who need a little help from volunteers. They don't have to go home together, but do enjoy each other's company.


I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!


The adoption fee is waived for Yerba and all Cactus Cats/Desperate Housecats!

name: Nayla
description_html:
Nayla is one special girl who will absolutely steal your heart from the very first hello. She’s friendly, fun, and full of affection, always ready for a splash in the pool or a good game of fetch. But don’t let her playful side fool you—she also knows how to slow down and get super cozy, especially when she’s wrapped up in her favorite blankets, just soaking up all the love from her people.


This sweetheart is house-trained and walks beautifully on a leash, making her an easy companion for both busy days and relaxed evenings. Nayla’s the perfect size for cuddles, adventures, and everything in between.


Her beautiful salt-and-pepper face gives her an undeniable old soul charm, and you can tell she’s patiently waiting to find her forever family—the perfect match who will cherish her just as much as she will them.


If you’re searching for a loyal, loving companion who thrives on connection and cuddles, Nayla might just be the one to fill your life with joy and warmth.

name: Cornelia
description_html:
Cornelia is a striking pup with cute ears and stylish white booties that contrast beautifully with her shiny black coat. Cornelia is a little shy with new people, but a total goofball once she lets loose. This spunky pup is the ultimate "best of both worlds" companion. She has a fun, playful side that shines bright on walks and outdoor adventures, always ready to show off her athletic, happy stride, playing fetch or splashing around in some water. But once the energy is burned, Cornelia transforms into a professional cuddler. She loves nothing more than curling up close and melting into your lap. 

At 60 pounds, this 1-year-old is incredibly easy to handle and responds well to commands and walking on a leash. Cornelia is ready to bring endless joy and loyal companionship to her forever home. Looking for a fun sidekick for all your adventures? Cornelia is your gal!

name: Mr Kitten
description_html:
Built like a tank but with a heart as squishy as a land manatee, this big blockhead is impossible not to love.

Mr. Kitten has excellent leash manners and is wonderfully patient with newer volunteers—he’ll let beginners practice harnessing without a fuss. He’s calm and collected walking past kennels or other dogs, taking everything in stride with his signature mellow confidence.

Under that handsome gray coat is the sweetest heart you’ll ever meet. Don’t let his size fool you: he’s a gentle giant through and through. On the leash, he’s a loose-leash pro; off the leash, he becomes the world’s cuddliest couch companion with plenty of surface area for snuggles.

Kind, loving, and endlessly mellow, Mr. Kitten is the dog who wins people over instantly. Come meet him—your only regret will be not doing it sooner.


Additional Foster Notes

Wow, he's amazing! In addition to the notes above, he's rarely vocal, doesn't beg for food, allows bathing and grooming, he's potty trained, and he does well in crowds with screaming kiddos and music. He's a great car passenger and was fantastic with my 6 year old niece.


His ideal home would include a yard and maybe another dog friend, but he's happy anywhere he can be with his people. He has some separation anxiety, but I feel this might be remedied with time and feeling secure.


He may be okay with cats, but this requires time and close supervision for a while to be sure.


Austin Animal Shelter tested him as heartworm positive, but Emancipet tested months later prior to treatment and said he was clear.

name: Rocko
description_html:
Do you have a big heart for a sensitive dog who will make every little win feel huge?


JR came to the shelter after his owner had a change in housing, and the shelter has been a hard place for him. He is one of those dogs who feels everything deeply, and while he may not be the dog doing big, flashy things right away, he is exactly the kind of dog who will make your heart swell with every small, brave win. 


JR came in with a few other dogs and is now the only one still waiting. For his first few months, he kept to himself and did not quite want to leave his kennel. But little by little, he has been getting braver every day, taking new steps, trying new paths, and showing the kind of quiet progress that feels incredibly rewarding to witness. He is the kind of dog who reminds you to slow down and enjoy the little things. With JR, flourishing might look like a short walk, a tiny tail wag, or choosing to follow you somewhere new, and somehow those moments feel huge. 


He would love a gentle foster or adopter who can move at his pace and help him keep building confidence. JR is a sweet, soulful guy who is scared of the shelter environment but full of potential outside of it. Give him a chance, and you will get to watch him bloom.

name: Shamu
description_html: Shamu has been through some tough times and he was not the happiest camper at the shelter, so this lucky boy landed in foster care. He currently resides in a home with other cats and would also do fine as an only child. Shamu can be a hidey cat when he's not feeling brave. He may prefer a calmer home with a cat savvy person to set him up for success. He's not a great fit for small children or dogs. This boy could use a place to decompress and get his kitty life back on track!

name: Mae
description_html:
Meet Mae! She's a friendly girl who loves a game of fetch. Mae is very friendly once she gets to know you and will climb in your lap for cuddles and pets. 46 pounds isn't too big to be a lap dog, right? We don't think so! Mae gets along well with her current room mate at the shelter, too. She goes potty like a champ outside. What more can you ask for? Adopt Mae today!

Interested in Mae? Reach out to blueplusbusters@gmail.com for more info!

name: Sweet Pea
description_html:
Meet Sweet Pea

This cuddler and a charmer is home tested and has all the important skills expected of a mature lady, She's loving, attentive, very housetrained, mannered, rides well in the car. At less than 60 lbs she's smaller statured. She's spayed and ready to go home right away. .


Sweet Pea prefers not to cohabitate with other pets. Sweet Pea has had some past traumas and being in close proximity to other dogs at the shelter has been very unnerving for her. An ideal setting will be with a loving family in a home setting where she can find her zen. She's so ready for that. Are you ready to meet this pretty lady? Sweet Pea is advocated for by the orangedotcrew@gmail.com - reach out with questions or come by to meet her.

name: Beef
description_html:
Beef is a handsome young pup with cute puppy eyes that show all the love he has to give! Beef loves fun and games; he lives for fetch and a good romp with any ball!

Beef isn't just a playful goofball; he also shows his true gentlemanly side. He's already housetrained and walks like a pro, making him great on a leash. Plus, he's shown to be dog-friendly and done well in playgroups. 

Ready for a devoted, ball-loving companion to bring some joy to your life? Beef is ready for his forever home!


If you have any questions, please reach out to orangedotcrew@gmail.com

name: Yeti
description_html:
Looking for a calm, cuddly companion? Yeti is your guy! This sweet, gentle, and mellow pup has been at the shelter since July, patiently waiting for his perfect match.


Yeti is very house trained, leash trained, and has the perfect medium energy—always ready for a walk, then a nap and some cuddles. He loves food and treats and enjoys the company of other chill dogs.


Oh, and he’s kind of a star—Yeti made a TV appearance on FOX 7, charming everyone he met!

See the segment here: https://www.fox7austin.com/video/1087057


If you’re looking for a laid-back, loving best friend, Yeti’s your match. Come meet this handsome boy today and see why everyone’s falling for him!

name: Xena
description_html:
Get to know Xena......... a loving brindle babe!

Xena is very smart and quick learner with the help of treats. She knows sit, shake, down, and kisses. Treats and food are big motivators and help to distract her when necessary. She is low to medium energy and rarely barks.

Xena casually strolls on a leash, but does get excited when she sees squirrels and lizards. She doesn't seem bothered by passing people or other dogs at a distance. She understands "Let's go and This Way." Expect to see some zoomies in a backyard. She likes to chase soccer balls. She is also happy to sit outside with her human and people-watch.

Spending time with Xena inside - She is housetrained. She will self-entertain with toys of all kinds, not a picky girl. Give her a smoked beef bone with marrow, and she is the happiest dog. Loves, loves, loves belly rubs!!! She is at ease when being groomed (bath, nails, and ears).

Xena loves car rides and is an excellent passenger. She does not jump over seats in an SUV. She will alternate looking out the window and lying down.

Xena is a great catch! Check her out!

interested in Xena? Reach out to blueplusbusters@gmail.com for more info!

name: Mariah
description_html:
Good girl Mariah is back and ever-more-ready for some lucky adopters to scoop her up and make her part of their home! Mariah was one of the lucky dogs who participated in our new Enduring Service program! This program pairs a shelter dog with inmates at the Travis State Jail to complete a 12-week basic training course, and she was a rockstar there!


Mariah is a sweet, intelligent pup with a big heart and an even bigger love for people. She's friendly with everyone she meets and, after a little time to adjust, does well with most other dogs too. Mariah isn't shy about saying hello, she'll happily walk right up for pets and attention! She already knows several commands, including sit, down, stay, paw (both hands!), and touch, and she even has a cute "bang bang" trick. Like many smart dogs, she's especially motivated when treats are involved. She's potty trained and mostly kennel trained, making her a great fit for a home ready to continue reinforcing her good habits. Mariah truly shines when she's outside. She loves having space to run and play, and she does especially well off-leash in a secure area. A home with a big yard would be ideal for her active, playful nature. She tends to stay close and doesn't wander off, making her a wonderful companion for outdoor time. During playtime, Mariah can get a little excited with her toys, so gentle guidance or a quick distraction can help keep things calm and fun for everyone. With her loving personality and joyful spirit, Mariah is sure to bring happiness and companionship to any home lucky enough to have her.

name: Dennis
description_html:
Meet Dennis 🧡

Dennis is a sweet, easygoing guy who really shines once he gets a moment to settle in. He may need a few minutes to warm up when coming out of his kennel, but after that, his gentle and affectionate personality takes over.

In our living room set up at the shelter, Dennis has been a total pro—very housetrained, non-destructive, and happy to relax with a toy while soaking up attention and pets. He’s the kind of dog who’s just as content hanging out with you as he is entertaining himself nearby.

On leash, Dennis generally walks nicely and doesn’t need a harness. He settles into a comfortable, mostly loose-leash walk.

He’s also a smart and eager learner! Dennis is treat-motivated, knows “sit,” and will sometimes offer a paw—an extra cute bonus.

With a little patience at the beginning, Dennis shows you just how loving and laid-back he really is. He’s ready to be someone’s loyal, chill companion.

name: Sandler
description_html:
If Sandler had a dating profile, we're pretty sure it would look something like this: 

About me: Hi there, I'm Sandler! Hoping to find a handsome, playful and cuddly young man you can introduce to your friends and family? Here I am. Seeking someone who shares your passion for an active lifestyle and highly trainable? Ding ding - you found him. Looking for someone who is incredibly good looking with a heart of gold? Look no further. My personality is even better than my handsome looks.

Looking for: I'm in it to win it. I want a lifelong companion. Someone who I can plant roots with (don't worry, I won't dig them up), go on adventures with and grow old with. Already started a family and have kids, the human or furry kind? That won't scare me away. I love other dogs, both male and female and love to play. 

Ideal Date: A long walk or play session in the yard with my toys, taking a quick dip in any body of water, a puddle will do, and then getting loved on by my human. Who of course gives me snacks. To wrap up the date, we'd go back inside and Netflix and Nuzzle. I'll even let you pick the show as long as I can snuggle up, get some scratches and take a cozy snooze.

Think this could be it, the true love you've been looking for? Swipe right, aka come meet me at the shelter today!

name: Marshall
description_html:
Marshall has just returned from a foster home stay packed full of love, loads of great memories and knowledge we can share with you. See his foster story here

He shared his adventures with a human friend and a dog friend for a few weeks in a high rise building in the urban center of Austin, taking the elevator that led to all the fun outdoor stuff like car rides,walks and hikes, water play, sniff walks. At home he's simply the supremo couch potato, burrito style cuddler.. He's already learned all his great indoor manners-he's house trained, crate trained, he understands what's expected of him. The shelter will even host a doggie meetup if you have a dog friend at home for him. He's been briefly cat tested and we think he's open to sharing a home with a cat. Marshall is a great size for many different homes. He has a great support network ready to assist so hurry to meet him in person or reach out to caring@classiccanines.org for a personalized experience.

name: Barry
description_html:
Barry is a lively, curious explorer who treats every day like a brand-new adventure. He loves his walks and will lead the way, sniffing out every new scent, making him the perfect partner for active hikes or morning strolls.

Barry has shown us just how inquisitive he is: loving enrichment puzzles, spelunking under couch covers for treasures, and even helping himself to a few milk bones. He’s a bundle of energy who loves exploring every nook and cranny.

But don’t let his playful antics fool you—Barry has a sweet, affectionate side. Once he’s had his fun, he’s more than happy to settle in next to you for pets and cuddles. He thrives on attention and companionship, making him a wonderful match for someone who enjoys both adventure and downtime.

Barry is ready to bring joy, laughter, and love into his forever home. Could that be with you?

name: Hank
description_html:
Introducing Hank and his heart of gold! 


When I first met this sweet boy he was very shutdown and scared in the shelter environment. He would cower in the back of his kennel and it took a lot of encouragement from his friends to even go for a walk. 

Since bringing him home to foster, Hank has beautifully opened up and shown the goofy, loving boy that he’s had inside all along. He’ll get so excited when you get home from work and then cuddle up next to you on the couch. All he wants is your love :)


Hank is a pup that will require patience, a gentle touch and an equally tender heart. He’s still learning to be brave in the world and it will take time, especially in a new home, but trust me seeing him blossom is a real gift. And the love he gives his people is fierce and unconditional!


Hank facts:

  • House-trained
  • Can free roam 
  • Quiet boy, very rare to hear him bark
  • Low to medium energy 
  • Expert snuggler!
  • Dog friendly - in fact he would love a calm yet confident dog friend to show him the ropes 
  • Loves a bully stick & cheese
  • Would love a back yard to play fetch (just a bonus!)
  • 100% angel 


Hank is a very special dog and he can’t wait to find his forever home! Is that you?

name: Snorkel
description_html:
Hi! I'm Snorkel, a bundle of joy who loves to explore every nook and cranny.


Besides being the most handsome boy, I'm playful and friendly, always ready for a game of fetch or a brisk walk. Curiosity drives me, and I'm pretty smart too, quickly figuring out puzzles and games. Independent yet loving, I thrive on a mix of adventure and quiet time. If you're looking for a loyal companion who's always up for a new challenge, I'm your guy!


Let's explore the world together. Adopt me and let the adventures begin!


Snorkel is neutered and ready to leave today!

name: Paxton
description_html:
Meet Paxton

As a fun, mature guy, he knows his true loves, being around his people, quiet time with his stuffies and chews, easy relaxing & leisure napping, having a roll in the grass, taking a sploot in the sunshine and if walks are your thing, he's all in for that!


Paxton's very housetrained (never marking!), stays home alone well, and is respectfully QUIET for the sake of your work at home schedule, and your neighbors. He also shares a relaxed pace with a calm dog friend, a 15 yr old Aussie.


Does this handsome man seem to set your heart a flutter? Connect on his link to chat with his foster or write his advocates at caring@classiccanines.org

name: Tonka
description_html:
Tonka is a pure bundle of joy who truly loves everything life has to offer: toys, walks, treats, water, and especially his beloved people friends! He could even join a family with a well-matched canine companion after a meet & greet with shelter staff.

One thing that sets Tonka apart is that he is deaf, which a volunteer noted is a huge asset! During his overnight stay, he "did so so good," adapting quickly to a new home and enjoying outings to sports bars and a friend's house. Being deaf helped him stay calm and stress-free in normally loud environments.

Deaf dogs are trained using hand signals and visual cues with positive reinforcement, and they live just like hearing dogs! For safety, they should always be leashed outside a securely fenced yard. Adopting a deaf dog like Tonka is incredibly rewarding, and there are many resources available to help you.

Tonka is well-behaved, quickly adapts, and is ready to shower his new family with unconditional love!

Tonka is a happy dog who enjoys playing with toys and splashing in the pool.

name: Whitley
description_html:
Whitley is a sweet and adorable girl who is full of love and energy! She's friendly with everyone she meets and adores being close to her humans, cuddling up whenever she can.

She loves playing fetch, chasing toys, and showing off her incredible zoomies. A yard or an active adopter would be a dream for her. She also has the potential to share her forever home with a dog sibling.

Whitley was being cared for by our medical team for the beginning of her stay with us, so we think this petite lady was just overlooked! But now she's happy, healthy, and ready to start her life and leave her kennel behind.

If you have any questions, please reach out to orangedotcrew@gmail.com to kickstart this heartwarming journey. For more delightful updates, follow Orange Dot Crew on Facebook and Instagram.

name: Rocky
description_html:
Meet Rocky


He's an adorable budding prince. You'll find him waiting by his kennel, with a smile and a tilted head because he really wants to connect with you. He hopes you might stop at his kennel, and stay more than a couple seconds to meet him. He's very smart, a craft in the making, and he looks for guidance from his person. His VIP will help him finesse his leash skills, after all he's just a youngster still learning. He has the foundation of becoming a heart dog for his person for a lifetime.


Rocky is neutered, heartworm negative, and ready to roll on out the door today!

name: Emmitt
description_html:
Our boy Emmitt has loved going on adventures outside of the shelter, and it’s helping us learn more about this very good boy. He loves parks and swimming. He went to a local gym so he could meet potential adopters. He greeted about 25 people, including a few kiddos. He loves to snuggle and play and hasn’t met a stranger.


Inside a home we found him to be so loving. He enjoys having a variety of toys to play with, but doing so right next to his people. He’s a multitasker who likes to play and snuggle at the same time. His signature move on a couch is to put his butt in the air and his head in your lap. This sweetheart is a bouncy ball of fun. At the shelter he loves going for walks, playing fetch in the play yards, or snuggling in the real life rooms.


Emmitt is also very house trained and knows a few tricks. He’s always up for a snack which make adding new tricks to his resume a breeze. He's met older kids and is so sweet.


We’d love to see Emmitt in a home with daily walks, a bit of fetch, a full belly, and some extra snuggles.

name: Walter
description_html: Meet Walter! Looking for the ultimate cuddle buddy? Walter is your guy! This sweet, gentle boy is the biggest lap dog you'll ever meet-he'll happily trade playtime for snuggles any day. Walter is a dream on the leash, making walks calm and easy, and he absolutely adores spending time with his volunteer friends. With his loving nature and gentle heart, Walter is ready to bring joy and comfort to a forever home. He's the kind of dog who will always be by your side, whether you're out for a stroll or curled up on the couch. Whoever gets to love Walter is going to be one very lucky person!

name: Lilly
description_html:
Meet Lilly – Your Gentle, Loyal Shadow 💕

Lilly will be looking for a new foster home beginning in June 2026. We have absolutely loved fostering her, and this sweet girl has a very special place in our hearts. Unfortunately, we’ll be moving and can’t bring her with us — so we’re hoping to find her next safe, loving landing spot.


Lilly is a shy girl at first, but once she feels safe, her loving personality shines. She bonds deeply with her people and absolutely adores cuddles, belly rubs, and being close to you. One of her favorite things is curling up next to you after a nice walk or ride in the car (yes — she loves car rides!).


She gains confidence from other dogs and truly blossoms in a home with canine companions. When she sees dogs she trusts being brave and happy, she quickly follows their lead. Because she spent much of her puppyhood in the shelter, having a confident dog friend helps her feel that the world is safe and fun. She has never shown any aggression toward dogs or people — she may bark occasionally when unsure, but it’s just her way of saying she needs a moment to feel comfortable.


Lilly is mostly housebroken. She prefers going outside to potty but will use puppy pads overnight if needed. She is crate trained and does well when crated, though she may need a little time to adjust in a new environment. For her comfort and safety, she should be crated or contained when left alone, and she does best when not left alone for more than a couple of hours at a time.


Her exercise needs are moderate — a fenced backyard or a couple of daily walks will keep her happy and healthy. She also loves treats and chew toys, which are great ways to bond with her. She loves playing fetch, so long as it isn't with a tennis ball.


Lilly’s ideal foster is patient, kind, and willing to let her open up at her own pace. In return, you’ll get to watch a shy little heart grow into an incredibly loyal and affectionate companion.


We would love to arrange at least one meet-and-greet with Lilly, our dogs, and her new foster before the transition to help her feel as comfortable as possible.


If you’re looking for a gentle, loving dog who just needs a little reassurance to shine, Lilly may be your girl. 💗

name: Fritz
description_html:
Fritz is a happy-go-lucky guy who brings good vibes everywhere he goes. Easygoing, well-adjusted, and always smiling, he rolls with whatever the day brings—walks, playtime, meeting new people—he’s just happy to be included.


He is a handsome boy who loves fetch and cuddles! He takes treats gently and is very good at sitting! 


He's an easy walker on a leash and doesn't pull. He also is good with kids -- he's met and played with some as young as 2 and is sweet with people.


If you’re looking for a dog with a great personality and an even better attitude who can adapt to a bunch of situations, Fritz would be an awesome companion.

name: Shelley
description_html: If you want your next pup to teach you calculus, coach you for your next marathon, and provide customized emotional support, look no further than Shelley! She has the personality, charm, good looks, and effervescent energy of ten dogs, and she can't wait to show you all her antics and share her cuddles with you. She'll greet you each day with a winning smile and teach you all the fun games she invents with her toys. She is an energetic bundle of joy wrapped in the cutest multicolored freckles you have ever seen! Don't sleep on Shelley; she is a dream of a dog!

name: Parker
description_html:
Parker is the kind of dog who leaves a lasting impression. At just 3 years old, he's affectionate, playful, and happiest when he's close to his favorite people—whether that's going for a walk, relaxing on the couch, or showing off just how handsome he looks in his pajamas. He has a charming goofy streak, a talent for making people smile, and firmly believes that no petting session should end before he's ready.

After spending more than a year in the shelter, Parker has become a favorite among volunteers. They've seen firsthand what a loving, loyal companion is, and they're eager to help him find the home he's been waiting for.

Parker recently spent a night on a sleepover with a volunteer and was an outstanding houseguest. He's completely house trained, slept through the night, had no accidents, walked nicely on leash, and left everything exactly as he found it. He showed wonderful house manners. 

Like many dogs who have spent a long time in a shelter, Parker may need a little time to adjust to a new environment. He's looking for someone who understands that trust and confidence don't always happen overnight. Parker has true “soul dog” potential and will just need a little empathy from his adopter or foster to help him transition into his new environment. 

Parker would be happiest in a quieter, lower-traffic home where he can settle into a predictable routine. 

If you're looking for a sweet, loyal dog with a playful personality, impeccable house manners, and a heart that's ready to belong to someone, Parker may be the perfect match. Bonus points if you appreciate a dog who looks especially distinguished in pajamas! 

Email orangedotcrew@gmail.com to learn more about Parker.

name: Bunny
description_html:
Meet Bunny

This sweet lady came to the shelter in urgent need of safety and care. She came from a tough situation and her condition was rough. She's spent the last few months on an amazing journey back to wellness, and to increased mobility. Bunny can now do everything that dogs do - besides being a great guest at home, she's house trained, she enjoys a little yard time and sunshine on her face. Short walks for exercise help her muscle strength. She's also a fish while guided in a swimming pool. She gives and receives so many affections. She's earned a reputation as a lap dog.


Bunny has a condition that makes her wobbly and unsteady on her feet, but she is aided by standard traction helpers like rugs, mats and being on flat surfaces when outdoors. Her Help Em Up harness also makes things a whole lot easier to guide her when needed. Bunny will do best in a single story arrangement as an only animal in her home.


Bunny's care includes occasional skin and eye management, and keeping her muscles in good tone. Her advocates at ClassicCaninesATX have been providing her care at an outside clinic and will help with supplies and some future veterinary care funding for an adopter.


Are you ready to make a difference in the life of a lady that has come this far already to prepare to meet you? Are you the person who will welcome her to unconditional forever love? Put her on your short list - and reach out on her link to talk with her foster provider or contact caring@classiccanines.org with questions about adoption support.


Watch her Pet of the Week highlight here!


See portions of Bunny's journey over the past few months here and here

name: Oscar
description_html:
Oscar, with his handsome brindle coat, is a sweet boy with a love for adventure! This pup thrives on exploration, whether he's hitting the trails, engaging in puzzle toys, or sniffing out new scents and romping around on walks. Oscar also loves kongs, squeaky toys, and squirrel watching through the window!

Oscar is a playful and loyal dog, ready for his forever home and someone or a family who can help him build his confidence through fun new experiences. He may be a little shy with unfamiliar things, but that quickly melts away with a few delicious treats and a dose of love. He'll soon be cuddling up with you, ready to be your devoted best friend!  


Interested in Oscar? Reach out to blueplusbusters@gmail.com for more info!

name: Bear
description_html:
Baron is a volunteer favorite. He's been going on lots of adventures lately and we've learned a bit more about this boy.


He loves car rides and knows "want to go for a car ride". He also knows "want to go to the park". He's great on leash and isn't reactive to any pedestrian traffic (strollers, bikes, dogs, etc).


He's house trained and knows basic commands like sit and shake. Baron loves all things food which makes learning new commands a breeze.


He's one of the more confident dogs when out and about (he's not startled by loud sounds or neighborhood construction). He loves all bodies of water though is still getting used to pools.


At first Baron isn't a cuddly dog that wants pets and prefers to keep touch to a minimum. Once a relationship has been established though he isn't shy to ask for some love!


He's previously lived with a cat but can have a rowdy play style with them. Slow introductions and initial separation would be needed.


Baron can live in a home or an apartment. If you like being active, going on adventures, going for car rides, and the water - he is your boy!

name: Patty Melt
description_html:
Meet Patty Melt


Patty Melt has the most adorable face and soulful brown eyes.

What can he do for you?

He's an easy home friend - very housetrained, quiet, relaxed and easy going.

He loves cuddle time with his people.

He walks nicely on a leash and if you're into walks, he's a great dog to have along.

Intelligence built in, he's well cued with his sit, shake, down and he takes treats nicely too!


Ready to check him out as your next companion? Click the link to alert his foster.

He's advocated for by his friends at caring@classiccanines.org who offer extra support for adopters. Find out why mature dog adoption is such an easy seamless fit for people of all ages.

name: Alfred
description_html: Alfred is a smart, active, and incredibly sweet boy who's eager to learn and loves having a job to do. He's fully housetrained, knows several words (especially "treat" and "food"), and will do just about anything for a piece of cheese. He can be a little shy when meeting new people, but once he warms up, he's loyal, affectionate, and very devoted. Alfred is social with other dogs, does well with cats, and enjoys playing fetch, zooming around the yard, and keeping watch from his favorite window. Athletic and graceful, Alfred runs like a gazelle but is just as happy soaking up ear scratches and butt rubs. With his brains, heart, and personality, he's ready to be a wonderful companion for the right family. 🐾

name: Phoenix
description_html:
Phoenix is a sweet, joyful boy with a heart full of love and curiosity. He’s been thriving in the play yard, where he enjoys long walks, sniffing everything around him, and splashing in the pool. Phoenix is a strong, energetic dog who loves keeping an eye on squirrels and birds, patrolling his surroundings with curiosity and excitement. He also absolutely loves playing fetch — a yard or outdoor space would be a huge benefit for him.

While not the cuddliest, Phoenix shows affection in his own way, giving gentle kisses and leaning into those moments when he wants attention. He’s easy to harness, eager to explore, and quickly warms up to people, radiating sweetness despite what seems to have been a difficult past. His face and fur are healing beautifully, and his resilient, happy nature shines through every interaction.

Phoenix is looking for a family who will celebrate his energy, patience, and loving spirit — someone who can give him space to run, sniff, and play while appreciating the gentle soul he is. Always smiling, Phoenix is truly a boy who leaves a lasting impression.

Ready for a rise through the adventure of life.

🌟Phoenix is also a celebrity after being featured on CBS Austin - check out his visit!

name: Sprinkles
description_html:
Meet Sprinkles! 


Sprinkles is a beautiful 2-year-old pup who’s ready to find her forever family. She’s spent time in a home before, so she already knows what life outside the shelter can look like—and she’s hoping her next chapter is the best one yet.


She enjoys the company of other dogs and could do well with a canine sibling. On walks, Sprinkles can cruise along nicely on leash but may pull when she gets excited—especially if there’s something interesting ahead! The good news? She’s very treat-motivated, which makes training both effective and fun.


A naturally shy girl, Sprinkles is searching for a patient, loving adopter who will help her build confidence at her own pace. With gentle guidance, structure, and plenty of encouragement, she has so much potential to shine in a home environment—and with the right match, she’ll blossom into a loyal, devoted companion.

name: Toby
description_html:
Some dogs make your life busier.

Toby somehow makes it quieter.

He has a way of melting beside you at the end of the day that makes your shoulders drop, your phone stay face-down a little longer, and your couch feel like exactly where you’re supposed to be. He’s happiest simply being with his people—whether that’s on a neighborhood sniffari, watching TV, or taking an afternoon nap while you work nearby.

Toby is house-trained, calm in the home, and incredibly devoted once he knows you’re his person.

After growing up in the shelter, he still appreciates gentle introductions and predictable routines. But when he feels safe, his whole personality unfolds: the happy wiggles, the goofy stretches, the soft sleepy sighs, and the quiet companionship that makes a house feel more like home.

All Toby really wants is someone to build an ordinary life with… but we have a feeling he’ll make y’all’s future feel pretty extraordinary.

name: Lady
description_html: Lady is a playful, sweet, loving Black Mouth Cur mix. She is very housetrained and looooves people! Lady loves to go on walks, run around the play yard, play fetch, and say hi to every human she sees. She loves her emotional support tennis balls. A lover of all pools. She is a playful pup in the play yard and then can be the calm cuddle partner in a home. She is gentle with toys and treats. She is easy to harness and get out of her kennel, she does have some dog reactivity - but she can be EASILY redirected, she is not one to linger. Super smart and can play fetch for hours if you'd let her. She is treat motivated, very curious, and eager to learn new tricks and please her human. Lady is a snuggly, cuddly girl, and very affectionate. This playful, loving girl loves to sit in her human's lap and give all the kisses and she'd prefer to be your one and only so she can soak up ALLL the love you have to give. Lady is an all around gem and is ready and waiting for her forever home and people to love on! Come meet Miss Lady girl today, she's waiting for you!

name: Dandelion
description_html:
Dandelion is a playful, affectionate, and downright joyful companion. At two years old, this cuddlebug and zoomie enthusiast is soaking up every bit of life on the outside—and he’s ready to share it all with his forever family.

Dandy is a social pup who loves chasing balls, splashing in a kiddie pool, going for walks, and tagging along on whatever adventure the day brings. When it’s time to relax, he’s all about the snuggles—curling up beside you and soaking in the love like it’s his favorite treat.

He gets along well with other dogs and is doing wonderfully with potty training, going up to 8 hours accident-free during the day and even longer overnight. He sleeps soundly on a couch, dog bed, or right next to you if invited.

Dandelion walks beautifully on a harness and shorter leash, exploring the world at your side with curiosity and calm. He’s smart, eager to engage, and channels his energy into all the right things—like enrichment toys, frozen carrots, stuffed Kongs, Chuck-It balls, and durable chews. He redirects easily and is always up for learning.

Because of an old vocal cord injury, Dandy doesn’t bark loudly—but he makes up for it with happy wiggles, soulful eyes, and gentle energy that speaks volumes. He’ll thrive with continued consistency, love, and either leashed adventures or a secure yard to safely enjoy the outdoors.

Dandelion is that rare blend of fun-loving and affectionate, making him the perfect hiking buddy, poolside pal, and loyal snuggle partner. He’s bloomed beautifully—now all he needs is a home where he can keep growing, loved and safe.


If you have any questions, please reach out to orangedotcrew@gmail.com to kickstart this heartwarming journey. For more delightful updates, follow Orange Dot Crew on Facebook and Instagram.

name: Cranberry
description_html: The heart on her nose is no joke, Cranberry is truly full of love. If you're looking for a companion to greet you like you're in the honeymoon phase for life, this is your girl. If you're looking for a girl who trots right by your side, this is your girl. If you're looking for a calm, peaceful, and aware presence while exploring the outside world together, this is your girl. If you're looking for a partner who can be nervous at times, but calmed but your validating words, pets, and built trust, this is your girl. If you're looking for a pet who gets excited about adventures and just being together, this is your girl. If you're looking for a pup who appreciates human company, company of other nice dogs, chew toys, walks, close snuggles, treats with tricks and great listening skills, this is your girl. If you're looking for a young dog who is beautiful inside and out, this is your girl.

name: Bruce
description_html:
Looking to add more joy, presence, and sunshine to your life?

Then it’s time to stop and smell the roses… and meet Bruce!

Welcome to the wonderful world of Bruce — a place where belly rubs are currency, fetch is a national sport, and love is always in the air. To enter, you’ll need just a few essentials:

✔️ Hands for belly rubs

✔️ Tennis balls for endless games of fetch

✔️ A pocket full of treats (because Bruce is a firm believer in snack-based diplomacy)

Bruce is a handsome, high-energy guy who’s all about his people and living life to the fullest. Whether it’s hiking trails, exploring new parks, or just being your co-pilot for the everyday moments, Bruce is always up for the ride. He’s dog-selective, so slow intros or a solo-pet home may be best—but when it comes to humans, he’s all in.

Bruce is such a sweetheart! He loves his friends, but it takes time to get to know him and his little quirks. He is a dog whose trust you must earn… he is cautious and that is completely acceptable. He has been in the shelter far too long and needs a person/family who is dependable, loving and loyal. Humans who will go slow and get to know him instead of forcing him to go at their place. He is a go-slow kind of guy. Bruce is a Very GOOD Boy who deserves all the best things that life can offer. He deserves a family. He deserves happiness.

If you’re searching for a loyal sidekick, an enthusiastic adventure buddy, and a reason to smile every day, Bruce might just be your perfect match. Come join Bruce World—it’s full of tail wags, playful zoomies, and unconditional love.


Email blueplusbusters@gmail.com for more info!

name: Hero
description_html: Meet Hero, a gentle giant with a heart full of love! This big boy is a sweet and affectionate pup who just wants to be loved. While he can be a little timid at first, he is incredibly gentle and eager to please. Once he opens up, he is the ultimate cuddle companion, happily curling up on the couch for some quality snuggle time. He also has a playful side and likes to explore new toys. His curiosity makes every walk an adventure, as he takes in all the sights and smells around him. Despite his initial shyness, Hero is a quick learner and always ready to impress. He has already mastered "sit" and "stay" before meals, showing just how smart and willing he is to learn. He thrives on patience and kindness, and his confidence continues to grow every day. Whether he's out exploring or lounging at home, he is the perfect balance of playful and affectionate. If you're looking for a loyal, loving companion with a gentle soul, Hero is ready to steal your heart!

name: Stax
description_html:
Meet Stax. He is the kind of dog who makes a house feel more like home. Whether he’s supervising dinner from the kitchen floor, watching birds from the window, carrying sticks through the snow, or resting his head on a pillow for an afternoon nap, he simply loves being part of everyday life. He has an expressive face, an infectious smile, and a goofy streak that shines through in everything from enthusiastic toy de-stuffing sessions to joyful backyard zoomies.

Life in foster homes has shown just how naturally Stax settles into a routine. He’s house-trained, sleeps through the night, rides well in the car, and is content to relax while his people work or tackle chores. He’s also bright and eager to learn—he picked up “shake” in just a few minutes—and always appreciates earning a tasty reward. Whether you’re heading out for an adventure or enjoying a quiet evening at home, Stax is happiest when he gets to come along for the ride.

Stax has spent far too long waiting for a family, but that hasn’t stopped him from forming meaningful connections everywhere he goes. He’s shared his foster homes with respectful children, happily welcomed affection, and charmed everyone from neighbors to houseguests with his warm personality. Like any dog, he’ll appreciate patient introductions to new experiences, but once he feels at home, his true personality shines: loyal, affectionate, funny, and endlessly endearing.

If you’re looking for a best friend who will make you laugh, join you on adventures, and then happily curl up nearby at the end of the day, Stax is ready to fill that role.

name: Marina
description_html:
Meet Marina

You'll love Marina - she's sweet, sensitive, a cuddler, a player, a great all around companion. She's thrived in a multi-dog home, and in a home with just one other dog.

She's a great size and nice middle age of wisdom - you can take her just about everywhere but she also stays home alone very well.

Marina is being treated for a touch of anemia and will soon be healthy again if you don't mind waiting just a bit, we'll get it sorted out. Her advocates at ClassicCaninesATX are helping her meet her health goals. To learn more contact caring@classiccanines.org

name: Jack
description_html:
Jack's had a challenging life. His people couldn't care for him, which brought him to the shelter about a dozen times over the years. Jack learned how to cope and tolerate life's storms. To say the least, he's resilient. He's great with all people, little and big. Jack is now looking for his forever home as a senior dog. We would like for him to finally have a chance at a regular dog's life. He deserves that.

His friends at ClassicCaninesATX are ready to help him, now we just need the person who wants to make a difference in this happy guys' life. Jack doesn't have to accept life laying down in a shelter - he can have a good life outside of the shelter walls, a time very well spent.

Jack loves people best, big families, little families, people of all ages. He's a champion snuggler and TV watcher and would love a spot simply hanging out in a living room. He's not a fan of cats at all but has some light dog experience and could work well with a calm, easy dog in the home- we can schedule a doggie meet up at the shelter.

Time is on the clock for Jack so don't miss out and don't skip over Jack if you're ready to help change a life forever more to give Jack the stable home life he's never had.

name: Teddy Bear
description_html:
He's well rounded, smart, wise, relaxed about life, and listens well. He's perfectly house trained and stays home alone well for long hours with his favorite independent activities, playing and napping with his toys. He loves affections when you are ready to dish them out and he's not demanding or pushy for them, he'll wait until your free. He's healthy too and just an overall easy dog to manage in a home.


Do you like the company of a dog to ride along for quick trips? He absolutely adores that - burgers, coffee runs and more.


Here's a couple things that really sing to his heart;

A home with a fenced yard, or if no yard, peaceful walks

He loves the peace of being an only pet but if you have a resident dog and would like to see how they hit it off, we're happy to schedule a dog meetup at the shelter.


Teddy's the kind of guy who's there for you through thick and thin, and without requiring a lot of fuss and over sight. Why not give him a try? Foster trial to adopt is an option too.


His adoption comes with extra support for a fresh stash of toys and a future vet care credit so he's easy to get established into a new home, all from his advocates at ClassicCaninesATX


He can't wait to meet you! Inquire on his link to speak with his foster about what he can do for you or write caring@classiccanines.org

Don't let this one slip by!

name: Meshe
description_html:
Meshe is a fun loving girl. She has the best smile that'll brighten your day! Meshe did very well in her foster home. She loved cuddling with her foster mom and her foster sibling. She likes to sleep a lot!

Meshe loves playing with squeaky toys and chewing on elk antlers, but is definitely not a fan of small animals. So, she'd be best with dog only families and no cats or rabbits. Reach out to the behavior team to set up a meet and greet with your dog(s) today.

Meshe also enjoys outings and car rides. She behaves well in the car, happily lying in the backseat, looking out the window.

Meshe would be a great hiking buddy. She really enjoys a good adventure! Her only hang up is no water sports. She's content to run around with her toys instead.

Meshe is a long stay pup that been fortunate to be living in a foster home these past 2 years, but her wish would be to find a loving, forever home with another dog to play with. Meshe is a cuddler and would love to snuggle next to you in bed. She truly is a joy to be around. Meshe will make you laugh and steal your heart.

name: Trap
description_html:
Affectionately called "Dorito Ears,"


Trap is very smart and knows the common commands. He would also want to let you know that he would love to learn more from you! His favorite things in the world are discovering balls in the yard and his friends.


This cuddle bug would like an medium/active energy owner. By this we mean, you either love walks or would fit him into your gardening time in the yard. He would know how to self entertain with balls/toys as you garden, but sometimes would come in check with you to make sure you know he's still your garden pal.


Treat motivated and Dog selective, Trap can be friends with his peers with proper introductions. He trusts the humans he knows and will slowly warm up to strangers.


Once he gets some of his energy out, Trap is very content just relaxing, check in for some pets, napping, and playing with his toys. He also loves to snuggle. Trap is very house trained and rides well in the car. He is a very smart cookie and loves puzzle games too! He will thrive with an active person who will engage his body and his brain.


Come meet Trap soon! If you have any questions, please reach out to orangedotcrew@gmail.com to kickstart this heartwarming journey.


If you've read this far, here are some unique quirks Trap has. He is afraid of sizzling noise of kitchen cooking, curious about dish washer, and is not a fan of the vacuum sounds. Will gather most of his toys into a place he's identified as his safe space / nap spot / or eating place. Knows the words home, find it, and inside + up/down. Understands Let's go/ walkie. Has a strong sit and lay down command is still being worked on.

name: Jade
description_html:
Meet Jade 💚

If you’re looking for a big love bug with an even bigger heart, Jade is your girl.

Jade is an 9 year old pitbull who completely redefines what “senior” means. With a medium energy level more like a 5-7 year old dog, she enjoys a nice walk or a burst of play- but her true specialty? Being the ultimate cuddle companion. She is incredibly sweet, affectionate, and happiest when she’s snuggled up next to her people.

Jade is fully house trained, does well being home alone for extended periods, and is respectful of household rules (she’ll happily get off the furniture when asked—though she’d love an invite back up for snuggles!). She also loves car rides and is always ready to be your co-pilot.

A few of Jade’s favorite things:

🐾 Snacks (her love language!)

🐾 Cozy naps

🐾 Sunbathing

🐾 Walks & car rides

Jade adores kids and brings a fun, playful energy—but due to her size and enthusiasm, she may do best with older children who won’t be easily knocked over during playtime.

Jade would thrive as the only pet in the home, as she prefers to have all the love and attention to herself. She can be reactive to other dogs on walks, so a home with a backyard or space to stretch her legs would set her up for success.

On walks, Jade really shines once she knows and trusts her person. With her owner, she can sit and walk nicely at a heel. With new people, she may test boundaries and can pull (she is strong!), but she improves as that relationship builds.

Like many big, lovable goofballs, Jade can get a little overexcited at times and may try to hump when she’s extra happy but she responds well to redirection and structure. She is also a quiet dog & rarely barks.

The best part? Jade has no adoption fee and no known medical needs—just a heart full of love ready to give.

If you’re looking for a loyal, affectionate companion who will fill your home with warmth (and take up most of your couch—and your sunny spots), Jade might just be your perfect match. 💛
